figures.caption: FIG. 1: (a) Setup: incident circularly polarized light is scattered from a two-dimensional honeycomb ferromagnet. (b) Upper and lower magnon bands calculated from Eq. (17). (c,d) Berry curvatures of the upper (c) magnon band and the lower (d) magnon band. Parameters are taken from Ref. [44], and are J1 = 2.01 meV, J2 = 0.16 meV, J3 = −0.08 meV, Az = 0.49 meV, and D = 0.31 meV. The length of the spin vector is S = 3/2.

FIG. 2: Thermal Raman circular dichroism as a function of frequency, for a topological monolayer of the ferromagnet CrI3 for temperatures below the Curie temperature Tc = 45 K. (a) Results for different temperatures for D = 0.31 meV. The positive peak indicates the topological band gap at the K and K′ points. The negative broadband signal originates from the high thermal population at the energy minimum around Γ point. (b) Signal for different D at T = 35 K. The signal vanishes at D = 0, corresponding to the topologically trivial limit. Model parameters are taken from Ref. [44].
